mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in high PAYEMS periods fall below 0.5% in 60%+ of cases, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 200 signals persistent inflationary pressures that prompt central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in CPIAUCSL > 200 periods exceed 1% in 60%+ of cases, or the predictive power of CPIAUCSL drops below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals strong supply-side momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to explain more than 10% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows, or returns diverge in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS above 4.5% compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in FEDFUNDS > 4.5% periods exceed 1% in 60%+ of cases, or the predictive coefficient for FEDFUNDS drops below -0.05 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict SPX returns with a 0.15+ absolute correlation in 3-year rolling windows, or negative returns occur in <30% of INDPRO decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to correlate with SPX returns above 0.1 in 3-year rolling windows, or positive returns occur in <40% of HOUST rise periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real sales (RSAFS) and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL < 200) signals strong consumer demand without price pressures, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and CPIAUCSL explains <5% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows, or returns diverge in 40%+ of cases.
